purpose: Topology-aware T-shirt reset, metric preconditioning, a matched sleeve-layer topology stress, and an IPC-protected gravity/table release.

construction.label: Cotton-jersey half-sleeve T-shirt
construction.calibration_status: construction-complete engineering prior; no named T-shirt measured

notes: Body front and back are separated layers joined by welded construction seams.
Sleeves are open-cuff flattened tubes with cap, underarm, cuff, and fold-axis constraints.
The neck rib follows its stitched attachment and offsets toward the neck opening.
Layer separation is reset-only numerical preconditioning, not calibrated textile thickness.

report.note: Deterministic orthotropic edge-length preconditioner with an explicitly reported seed-height planarity penalty; Newton/Style3D settling and contact probes remain required.

metric_preconditioner.note: The exact seed-height term is reset-only numerical preconditioning and is absent from runtime dynamics.

protocol.scope_note: Synthetic, reset-only mechanism challenge; it establishes a topology-preservation distinction, not a real handling load or textile calibration claim.

penalty_only.rejection_reason: Style3D penalty proximity contact admits an exact local surface crossing in the matched folded-sleeve challenge.

audit.calibration_status: construction-complete; engine adapter verified structurally; material and seam coefficients remain prior-only
unsupported_terms: warp/weft tension stiffening is nonlinear in the authored profiles but Style3D 1.4 uses constant triangle stiffness
cross-coupling ratio is not exposed by the Style3D 1.4 cloth helper
pressure-dependent thickness/compression is not exposed by the Style3D 1.4 cloth helper
measured per-seam rest angle and moment-curvature are unavailable; the explicit structural-seam bending scale defaults to a free hinge instead of silently treating a seam as continuous fabric
Style3D 1.4 does not consume its stored damping fields; the adapter can explicitly apply objective membrane-rate and dihedral-rate damping
quadratic normal air drag omits textile permeability, surrounding-flow coupling, wakes, and lift

table_plane_ccd.method: local swept-sphere/top-plane TOI plus normal impulse

audit.calibration_status: construction-complete; engine adapter verified structurally; material and seam coefficients remain prior-only
unsupported_terms: warp/weft tension stiffening is nonlinear in the authored profiles but Style3D 1.4 uses constant triangle stiffness
cross-coupling ratio is not exposed by the Style3D 1.4 cloth helper

Style3D 1.4 does not consume its stored damping fields; the adapter can explicitly apply objective membrane-rate and dihedral-rate damping

table_plane_ccd_summary.method: local swept-sphere/top-plane TOI plus normal impulse

calibration_status: mechanism/topology gate only; textile, contact, friction, damping, and reset-separation values remain unmeasured engineering priors
known_limitations: IPC is operator-split rather than a monolithic incremental-potential solve.
Finite thickness remains Newton Style3D proximity contact; IPC dmin is zero at welded seams.
The local swept-particle table response is also operator-split; Newton supplies tangential Coulomb contact and no pressure-complementarity claim is made.
Exact intersection confirmation uses IPC Toolkit, while Newton private triangle-candidate internals require compatibility testing on upgrades.

The matched stress is a synthetic reset-only mechanism challenge, not robot actuation or a calibrated real handling load; its rejected crossing is topology-local within a folded sleeve rather than a claim of nonlocal whole-garment tunneling.
The topology-aware flat lay is a reset state, not evidence that a robot performed the fold.
Pressure-dependent thickness and measured garment calibration remain pending.
